_meta.description: Manifest of research-ready export batches for the SumoWatch unresolved profile verification cycle.
_meta.mergeWorkflow: For each completed batch, run: node scripts/merge-research.mjs --file <batch-file> [--apply]

batches.purpose: Verified-identity profiles — need division lookup only
Lower unknown block (indices 413–457) — deep lower-division territory

researchOrder.reason: Quick win. Binary outcome: wrestler exists or does not. Resolves the only quarantined profile.
Highest value/lowest risk. All 21 have verified identity — only need division lookup. Full biographical data already present.
Establishes the upper boundary of the big unknown block. Resolving 10 profiles here narrows the division range for batch D.
Mixed border zone. Independent of C/D — can be researched in parallel with C. Division mix expected (Makushita/Sandanme/Juryo).
Largest and lowest-confidence batch. Should be done last — resolving C first constrains the possible division range.

risks.note: If Tsushimanada cannot be found, the profile stays quarantined permanently. Impact: 1 profile.
2 outlier profiles (idx 354, 375) are outside the main 223–250 block and may be in unexpected divisions.
Original import batch crossed division boundaries. Each profile genuinely could be Makushita, Sandanme, or Juryo.
Small batch but neighbors show Makushita/Sandanme mixing. Some "stale-rank" notes suggest rank data may be outdated.
45 profiles with almost no resolved neighbors between them. Division span is wide (Sandanme → Jonokuchi). Expect the most surprises.
